etymology.description: 氣(氣) (qì, "air; gas; breath") + 泡(泡) (pào, "bubble; blister") = a bubble filled with air, specifically an air bubble.
etymology.interpretation: 氣(氣) (qì, "air; gas; breath") represents the air or gas that is enclosed within the bubble. 泡(泡) (pào, "bubble; blister") specifically refers to a small, spherical thin-walled enclosure of a liquid that can trap gas inside it. Together, 氣(氣) and 泡(泡) denote a bubble primarily filled with air or gas, hence an "air bubble."

examples.chinese: 水中有很多气泡(水中有很多氣泡)。
examples.pinyin: Shuǐ zhōng yǒu hěn duō qìpào.
examples.english: There are many air bubbles in the water.

examples.chinese: 气泡在阳光下闪闪发光(氣泡在陽光下閃閃發光)。
examples.pinyin: Qìpào zài yángguāng xià shǎn shǎn fāguāng.
examples.english: The air bubbles glisten in the sunlight.

examples.chinese: 他用吸管吹出气泡(他用吸管吹出氣泡)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng xīguǎn chuī chū qìpào.
examples.english: He blew air bubbles with a straw.
